name: F-16 Fighting Falcon
mission: multirole air superiority and ground attack
key_features: Single engine, bubble canopy, side-stick, fly-by-wire, relaxed static stability, cropped delta wing, 9-g capable, M61 cannon
design_philosophy: Lightweight Fighter program: small agile aircraft, high thrust-to-weight, energy-maneuverability theory, low cost, easy maintenance
short_description: American single-engine supersonic multirole fighter. Designed for day fighter evolved to all-weather. Most common fixed-wing military aircraft. Bubble canopy and reclined seat reduce g-force. First fighter purpose-built for 9-g maneuvers.

name: F-22 Raptor
mission: air superiority with ground attack and electronic warfare
key_features: Twin engine, stealth, supercruise (Mach 1.5 without afterburner), supermaneuverability, thrust vectoring, internal weapons bays, sensor fusion
design_philosophy: Advanced Tactical Fighter: dominate contested airspace, combine stealth with speed and agility, first to integrate supercruise and supermaneuverability
short_description: American twin-engine stealth air superiority fighter. First operational 5th-gen. Combines stealth, supercruise, supermaneuverability. Diamond delta wings, canted tails. Replaced F-15 in USAF squadrons.

name: Supermarine Spitfire
key_features: Elliptical wing, Rolls-Royce Merlin/Griffon engine, stressed-skin monocoque, thin wing cross-section, eight-gun armament
design_philosophy: Thinnest possible wing for speed, elegant aerodynamics from Schneider Trophy seaplanes, home defense interceptor
short_description: British single-seat fighter. Icon of Battle of Britain. Elliptical wing minimized induced drag. Evolved through many variants from Mk I to Mk 24. Only British fighter in continuous production throughout WWII.

name: SR-71 Blackbird
key_features: Mach 3.2+, 85000 ft altitude, titanium airframe, chines for lift and stealth, J58 engines, signals intel and cameras
design_philosophy: Speed and altitude as survivability: outrun missiles, fly above threats, minimal radar cross-section via shaping
short_description: American long-range Mach 3+ strategic reconnaissance aircraft. Operated at 85000 ft. Evaded threats by accelerating. Titanium construction for thermal expansion. Still holds speed and altitude records.

name: Lockheed U-2
key_features: Single engine, 70000+ ft ceiling, glider-like high aspect ratio wings, bicycle landing gear, specialized cameras
design_philosophy: Fly above enemy reach: altitude over speed, sailplane-inspired lightweight design, 70k ft beyond interceptors
short_description: American single-engine high-altitude reconnaissance. Dragon Lady. Designed for 70000 ft over Soviet Union. Glider-like 23:1 glide ratio. Narrow coffin corner at cruise. Difficult to fly and land.

name: F-117 Nighthawk
key_features: Faceted flat surfaces for radar deflection, subsonic, twin engine, internal weapons bay, fly-by-wire for unstable design
design_philosophy: First operational stealth: faceted surfaces scatter radar, subsonic to avoid sonic boom, attack not dogfight
short_description: American stealth attack aircraft. Faceted shape minimizes radar cross-section. Aerodynamically unstable requires constant fly-by-wire. First operational stealth design. Retired 2008, some kept for training.

name: B-52 Stratofortress
key_features: Eight turbojet engines, swept wings, 70000 lb weapons capacity, 8800 mile unrefueled range, flexible airframe
design_philosophy: Long-range heavy payload: outlast competitors, flexible design, low operating cost, conventional and nuclear
short_description: American subsonic strategic bomber. BUFF. In service since 1955. Carries 70000 lb of weapons. Long range without refueling. Used in Vietnam, Gulf War, Afghanistan. Expected to serve into 2050s.

name: A-10 Thunderbolt II
key_features: GAU-8 30mm cannon, titanium bathtub armor, straight wing, twin turbofan, high survivability, short rough-field takeoff
design_philosophy: Tank killer: built around cannon, survivability over speed, loiter time, operate from austere forward bases
short_description: American single-seat close air support. Warthog. Airframe designed around GAU-8 Avenger cannon. 1200 lb titanium armor protects pilot. Can absorb damage and continue. Short takeoff from unpaved runways.

name: Concorde
key_features: Ogival delta wing, four Olympus turbojets with reheat, droop nose, Mach 2 cruise, aluminium construction
design_philosophy: Double speed of sound: slender delta for supersonic efficiency, vortex lift for low-speed, premium travel
short_description: Anglo-French supersonic airliner. Mach 2 transatlantic. Ogival delta wing, variable intake ramps. First airliner with analogue fly-by-wire. Retired 2003. Only competitor was Tu-144.

name: F-4 Phantom II
key_features: Twin engine, Mach 2.2, two-seat, no internal cannon initially, nine hardpoints, 18000 lb weapons
design_philosophy: Multirole heavy fighter: speed and payload over agility, radar-guided missiles, fleet defense and strike
short_description: American twin-engine supersonic interceptor and fighter-bomber. Most produced American supersonic military aircraft. Set 15 world records. Vietnam War workhorse. Evolved for air superiority, ground attack, reconnaissance.

name: B-2 Spirit
key_features: Flying wing, subsonic, two crew, lambda wing planform, low observables, internal weapons, 6000+ nmi range
design_philosophy: Penetrate defended airspace: pure flying wing for stealth, no vertical surfaces, curved composite surfaces
short_description: American stealth strategic bomber. Flying wing design. Penetrates sophisticated air defenses. 6000+ nmi unrefueled range. Conventional and nuclear. Replaced B-1A. Only 21 built. Cost $2B+ each.

name: C-17 Globemaster III
key_features: Four turbofan engines, swept wing, STOL capability, rear ramp, airdrop, 170000 lb payload
design_philosophy: Strategic and tactical in one: large payload, short rough-field landing, replace C-141 and augment C-5
short_description: American military transport. Strategic and tactical airlift. Based on YC-15. Carries troops and cargo. Medical evacuation and airdrop. Replaced C-141. Operates from austere runways.

name: Hawker Siddeley Harrier
key_features: Vertical takeoff and landing, vectored thrust Pegasus engine, four swivelling nozzles, ski-jump launch
design_philosophy: First operational V/STOL: operate without runways, disperse from vulnerable bases, amphibious assault
short_description: British V/STOL ground-attack aircraft. First operational fixed-wing V/STOL. Pegasus vectored-thrust engine. Operated from carriers and forward bases. Falklands War. Evolved to Sea Harrier and AV-8B Harrier II.

name: F-15 Eagle
key_features: Twin engine, high thrust-to-weight (>1), bubble canopy, all-weather, Mach 2.5, 8 hardpoints
design_philosophy: Not a pound for air-to-ground: pure air superiority, outclimb and outaccelerate any threat, look-down shoot-down
short_description: American twin-engine air superiority fighter. Thrust-to-weight greater than one. Designed to counter Soviet fighters. Undefeated in air-to-air combat. Evolved to F-15E Strike Eagle for dual role.

name: F-14 Tomcat
key_features: Variable-sweep wing, two-seat, AIM-54 Phoenix missile, twin engine, AWG-9 radar
design_philosophy: Fleet defender: variable geometry for carrier and speed, long-range Phoenix, intercept at distance
short_description: American variable-sweep wing fleet defense fighter. Swing wings optimize for low-speed carrier and high-speed intercept. Phoenix missile for long range. Top Gun fame. Replaced by F/A-18.

name: C-130 Hercules
key_features: Four turboprop engines, high wing, rear ramp, STOL, 42000 lb payload, rough field capable
design_philosophy: Tactical workhorse: operate anywhere, reliable, versatile, airdrop and assault, 60+ years in production
short_description: American four-engine turboprop tactical transport. High wing, rear ramp. STOL from rough strips. Airdrop, assault, medevac, tanker variants. Most produced military transport. In production since 1954.

name: MiG-21
key_features: Delta wing, single engine, small radar cross-section, Mach 2, tailed delta, minimal frontal area
design_philosophy: Minimalist interceptor: small, fast, cheap, point defense, GCI-guided, mass production for Warsaw Pact
short_description: Soviet single-engine supersonic fighter. Most produced jet aircraft. Delta wing, tailed configuration. Lightweight point-defense interceptor. Used by 60+ nations. Simple and reliable.

name: Boeing 747
key_features: Four engines, hump upper deck, wide body, 400+ passengers, long range, cargo and passenger variants
design_philosophy: Jumbo Jet: double capacity, economy of scale, cargo capability, cockpit above main deck for nose loading
short_description: American wide-body commercial airliner. Jumbo Jet. First twin-aisle. Hump for cockpit and upper deck. Cargo nose door. Revolutionized long-haul travel. Queen of the Skies.

name: F-35 Lightning II
key_features: Single engine, stealth, sensor fusion, STOVL variant, internal weapons, distributed aperture system
design_philosophy: Joint strike fighter: one airframe three variants (conventional, carrier, STOVL), stealth multirole, international
short_description: American single-engine stealth multirole. F-35A conventional, F-35B STOVL, F-35C carrier. Sensor fusion. Replacing F-16, A-10, F/A-18, Harrier. International program.

name: C-5 Galaxy
key_features: Four turbofan engines, nose and rear loading, 265000 lb payload, outsize cargo, 2700 nmi range
design_philosophy: Heavy lifter: largest USAF cargo, main battle tanks, outsize loads, nose and tail doors for drive-through
short_description: American strategic heavy airlift. Largest military transport. Nose and rear loading. Carries main battle tanks, helicopters. Replaced C-141 for heaviest loads. C-5M modernized.

name: 3I Sky Arrow
key_features:
The Sky Arrow is a tandem two-seat, high-wing pusher light aircraft that was manufactured by 3I (Iniziative Industriali Italiane). 
3I entered bankruptcy proceedings in 2008, and in 2012 the design wa
short_description:
The Sky Arrow is a tandem two-seat, high-wing pusher light aircraft that was manufactured by 3I (Iniziative Industriali Italiane). 
3I entered bankruptcy proceedings in 2008, and in 2012 the design was purchased by Magnaghi Aeronautica, of Naples, Italy.

name: 3Xtrim 3X55 Trener
key_features: The 3X55 Trener (Trainer) and 3X47 Ultra are a family of ultralight aircraft produced in Poland by the 3Xtrim Aircraft Factory. Both are two-seat, high-wing, strut-braced monoplanes with fixed tricycl
short_description: The 3X55 Trener (Trainer) and 3X47 Ultra are a family of ultralight aircraft produced in Poland by the 3Xtrim Aircraft Factory. Both are two-seat, high-wing, strut-braced monoplanes with fixed tricycle undercarriage and available only as completed aircraft.

name: 3Xtrim Aircraft Factory
key_features:
The 3Xtrim Aircraft Factory, or Zakłady Lotnicze 3Xtrim Sp.z o.o.in Polish, was an aircraft manufacturer located in the city of Bielsko-Biała, in southern Poland.
The company was established by former
short_description:
The 3Xtrim Aircraft Factory, or Zakłady Lotnicze 3Xtrim Sp.z o.o.in Polish, was an aircraft manufacturer located in the city of Bielsko-Biała, in southern Poland.
The company was established by former SZD sailplane engineer Adam Kurbiel in 1996 under the name Wytwórnia i Naprawa Konstrukcji Lekkich - A. Kurbiel.

name: A-90 Orlyonok
key_features:
The A-90 Orlyonok (Russian: Орлёнок, lit. 'Eaglet') is a Soviet ekranoplan that was designed by Rostislav Evgenievich Alexeyev of the Central Hydrofoil Design Bureau.
The A-90 uses ground effect to fl
short_description:
The A-90 Orlyonok (Russian: Орлёнок, lit. 'Eaglet') is a Soviet ekranoplan that was designed by Rostislav Evgenievich Alexeyev of the Central Hydrofoil Design Bureau.
The A-90 uses ground effect to fly a few meters above the surface.

name: A-B Helicopters A/W 95
key_features:
The A-B Helicopters A/W 95 is an American helicopter, produced by A-B Helicopters in the form of plans for amateur construction.
By 2012 the A-B Helicopters website had been taken down and plans are n
short_description:
The A-B Helicopters A/W 95 is an American helicopter, produced by A-B Helicopters in the form of plans for amateur construction.
By 2012 the A-B Helicopters website had been taken down and plans are no longer available.

name: A-I-R Atos
key_features: The A-I-R Atos is a German high-wing, single-place, rigid wing hang glider designed by Felix Ruehle and produced by A-I-R GmbH of Halblech.
short_description: The A-I-R Atos is a German high-wing, single-place, rigid wing hang glider designed by Felix Ruehle and produced by A-I-R GmbH of Halblech.

name: A2 CZ Ellipse Spirit
key_features: The A2 CZ Ellipse Spirit (sometimes spelled Elipse Spirit) is a Czech ultralight aircraft, designed and produced by A2 CZ. The aircraft was introduced at the Aero show in 2011 and is supplied complete
short_description: The A2 CZ Ellipse Spirit (sometimes spelled Elipse Spirit) is a Czech ultralight aircraft, designed and produced by A2 CZ. The aircraft was introduced at the Aero show in 2011 and is supplied complete and ready to fly or as a kit for amateur construction.

name: A41 Factory VNS-41
key_features: The VNS-41 is the first amphibious microlight aircraft made in Vietnam. The A41 Factory (officially Aircraft Repair Company A-41) under the Air Force and Air Defence Department (Ministry of Defence) m
short_description: The VNS-41 is the first amphibious microlight aircraft made in Vietnam. The A41 Factory (officially Aircraft Repair Company A-41) under the Air Force and Air Defence Department (Ministry of Defence) manufactured the aircraft based on the Russian Che-22 Korvet design by Boris Chernov and E. Yungerov.

name: AAC Angel
key_features: The Angel Aircraft Corporation Model 44 Angel is a twin-engine STOL utility aircraft produced in the United States since the mid-1990s.  Designed by Carl Mortenson and The King's Engineering Fellowshi
short_description: The Angel Aircraft Corporation Model 44 Angel is a twin-engine STOL utility aircraft produced in the United States since the mid-1990s.  Designed by Carl Mortenson and The King's Engineering Fellowship to be well-suited for missionary work from remote locations around the world, it is a low-wing cantilever monoplane with a retractable tricycle undercarriage and eight seats.

name: AAC SeaStar
key_features: The AAC SeaStar is an amphibious biplane that was produced in kitplane form. The aircraft is built largely of composite materials and features wings that may be easily removed for transport, and a bal
short_description: The AAC SeaStar is an amphibious biplane that was produced in kitplane form. The aircraft is built largely of composite materials and features wings that may be easily removed for transport, and a ballistic recovery system in the form of a parachute that can be deployed from the engine nacelle.

name: AAC Seastar Sealoon
key_features: The AAC Seastar Sealoon is a Canadian amateur-built flying boat, under development by Amphibian Airplanes of Canada. The aircraft is intended to be supplied as a kit for amateur construction.
short_description: The AAC Seastar Sealoon is a Canadian amateur-built flying boat, under development by Amphibian Airplanes of Canada. The aircraft is intended to be supplied as a kit for amateur construction.

name: AAI Aerosonde
key_features: The AAI Aerosonde is a small unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) designed to collect weather data, including temperature, atmospheric pressure, humidity, and wind measurements over oceans and remote areas.
short_description: The AAI Aerosonde is a small unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) designed to collect weather data, including temperature, atmospheric pressure, humidity, and wind measurements over oceans and remote areas.  The Aerosonde was developed by Insitu, and is now manufactured by Aerosonde Ltd, which is a strategic business of AAI Corporation.

name: AAI Corporation
key_features: AAI Corporation is an aerospace and defense development and manufacturing firm, located in Hunt Valley, Maryland, US. Formerly a wholly owned subsidiary of United Industrial Corporation, AAI was acqui
short_description: AAI Corporation is an aerospace and defense development and manufacturing firm, located in Hunt Valley, Maryland, US. Formerly a wholly owned subsidiary of United Industrial Corporation, AAI was acquired by Textron in 2007. It currently operates as a unit of Textron Systems and employs more than 2,000.

name: AAI Penetrator
key_features: The American Aircraft International Penetrator was a military helicopter prototype conceived in 1990 as a gunship conversion of the Bell UH-1 Iroquois, aimed at ground insertion, close support and cou
short_description: The American Aircraft International Penetrator was a military helicopter prototype conceived in 1990 as a gunship conversion of the Bell UH-1 Iroquois, aimed at ground insertion, close support and counter-insurgency roles.

name: AAI RQ-2 Pioneer
key_features: The AAI RQ-2 Pioneer is an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) that was used by the United States Navy, Marine Corps, and Army, and deployed at sea and on land from 1986 until 2007. Initially tested aboard
short_description: The AAI RQ-2 Pioneer is an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) that was used by the United States Navy, Marine Corps, and Army, and deployed at sea and on land from 1986 until 2007. Initially tested aboard USS Iowa, the RQ-2 Pioneer was placed aboard Iowa-class battleships to provide gunnery spotting, its mission evolving into reconnaissance and surveillance, primarily for amphibious forces.

name: AAI RQ-7 Shadow
key_features: The AAI RQ-7 Shadow is an American unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) used by the United States Army, Australian Army, Swedish Army, Turkish Air Force and Italian Army for reconnaissance, surveillance, and
short_description: The AAI RQ-7 Shadow is an American unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) used by the United States Army, Australian Army, Swedish Army, Turkish Air Force and Italian Army for reconnaissance, surveillance, and target acquisition and battle damage assessment. Launched from a trailer-mounted pneumatic catapult, it is recovered with the aid of arresting gear similar to jets on an aircraft carrier.

name: AAI Sparrowhawk
key_features: The AAI SparrowHawk is an American two-seat pusher ultralight autogyro, available in kit form for amateur construction.
short_description: The AAI SparrowHawk is an American two-seat pusher ultralight autogyro, available in kit form for amateur construction.

name: ABC Motors
key_features: ABC Motors Limited ("All British (Engine) Company") of Hersham, Surrey, England was a manufacturer of cars, aircraft, motor scooters, and engines for road and air. Established by Ronald Charteris in H
short_description: ABC Motors Limited ("All British (Engine) Company") of Hersham, Surrey, England was a manufacturer of cars, aircraft, motor scooters, and engines for road and air. Established by Ronald Charteris in Hersham, Surrey in 1912, its chief designer was the young and talented Granville Bradshaw.

name: ABC Robin
key_features: The ABC Robin was a British single-seat light aircraft designed by A. A. (Tony) Fletcher in 1929. It was a high-wing, single-seat monoplane of conventional taildragger configuration.
short_description: The ABC Robin was a British single-seat light aircraft designed by A. A. (Tony) Fletcher in 1929. It was a high-wing, single-seat monoplane of conventional taildragger configuration.

name: ABS Aerolight
key_features: ABS Aerolight was a French aircraft manufacturer based in Sérignan-du-Comtat. The company specialized in the design and manufacture of powered parachutes and roadable aircraft, in the form of kits for
short_description: ABS Aerolight was a French aircraft manufacturer based in Sérignan-du-Comtat. The company specialized in the design and manufacture of powered parachutes and roadable aircraft, in the form of kits for amateur construction and ready-to-fly aircraft for the European Fédération Aéronautique Internationale microlight category.

name: ABS Aerolight Legacy
key_features: The ABS Aerolight Legacy is a French powered parachute that was designed and produced by ABS Aerolight of Sérignan-du-Comtat. Now out of production, when it was available the aircraft was supplied as
short_description: The ABS Aerolight Legacy is a French powered parachute that was designed and produced by ABS Aerolight of Sérignan-du-Comtat. Now out of production, when it was available the aircraft was supplied as a complete ready-to-fly-aircraft and as a kit for amateur construction.

name: ABS Aerolight Navigathor
key_features: The ABS Aerolight Navigathor is a French powered parachute and roadable aircraft that was designed and produced by ABS Aerolight of Sérignan-du-Comtat. Now out of production, when it was available the
short_description: The ABS Aerolight Navigathor is a French powered parachute and roadable aircraft that was designed and produced by ABS Aerolight of Sérignan-du-Comtat. Now out of production, when it was available the aircraft was supplied as a complete ready-to-fly-aircraft and as a kit for amateur construction.

name: ABS Aircraft
key_features: ABS Aircraft AG is a Swiss aircraft manufacturer, building Fournier-designed motorgliders. It was founded in Küsnacht (ZH) in 1985.
short_description: ABS Aircraft AG is a Swiss aircraft manufacturer, building Fournier-designed motorgliders. It was founded in Küsnacht (ZH) in 1985.

name: AC Mobil 34
key_features: AC Mobil 34 is a small French aircraft manufacturer based in Saint-Florentin. The firm produces a light aircraft in kitplane form.
short_description: AC Mobil 34 is a small French aircraft manufacturer based in Saint-Florentin. The firm produces a light aircraft in kitplane form.

name: AC Mobil 34 Chrysalin
key_features: The AC Mobil 34 Chrysalin is a two-seat light aircraft produced in France in kitplane form. The aircraft was designed to comply with the Fédération Aéronautique Internationale microlight rules.
short_description: The AC Mobil 34 Chrysalin is a two-seat light aircraft produced in France in kitplane form. The aircraft was designed to comply with the Fédération Aéronautique Internationale microlight rules.

name: ACAZ C.2
key_features: The ACAZ C.2, Ateliers de Construction Aeronautique de Zeebruge, was a prototype Belgian biplane fighter aircraft built in the 1920s.
short_description: The ACAZ C.2, Ateliers de Construction Aeronautique de Zeebruge, was a prototype Belgian biplane fighter aircraft built in the 1920s.

name: ACAZ T.2
key_features: The ACAZ T.1  and T.2, very similar to each other, were the first Belgian all-metal aircraft, flown in 1924. They were single-engined touring monoplanes which seated two.
short_description: The ACAZ T.1  and T.2, very similar to each other, were the first Belgian all-metal aircraft, flown in 1924. They were single-engined touring monoplanes which seated two.

name: ACS-100 Sora
key_features: The ACS-100 Sora is a Brazilian two-seat Light Sport Aircraft, designed by Advanced Composites Solutions.
short_description: The ACS-100 Sora is a Brazilian two-seat Light Sport Aircraft, designed by Advanced Composites Solutions.

name: ACS-Itaipu Sora-E
key_features: The Sora-E is a two-seat electric powered aircraft. It is a joint venture between ACS Aviation of Brazil and Itaipu Binacional of Paraguay.
short_description: The Sora-E is a two-seat electric powered aircraft. It is a joint venture between ACS Aviation of Brazil and Itaipu Binacional of Paraguay.

name: AD Aerospace
key_features:
AD Aerospace are involved in Aircraft Video Surveillance and form part of the Mythra Group of companies.
Headquartered in Hazel Grove, Cheshire, UK at purpose-built premises, making Flight Deck Entry/
short_description:
AD Aerospace are involved in Aircraft Video Surveillance and form part of the Mythra Group of companies.
Headquartered in Hazel Grove, Cheshire, UK at purpose-built premises, making Flight Deck Entry/Cockpit Door Surveillance Systems (FDEVSS/CDSS), Cabin Surveillance Systems and Cargo Surveillance Systems, as well as Ground Manoeuvring and In Flight Entertainment (IFE) systems utilising external c...

name: AD Flying Boat
key_features: The AD Flying Boat was designed by the British Admiralty's Air Department to serve as a patrol aircraft that could operate in conjunction with Royal Navy warships. Intended for use during the First Wo
short_description: The AD Flying Boat was designed by the British Admiralty's Air Department to serve as a patrol aircraft that could operate in conjunction with Royal Navy warships. Intended for use during the First World War, production of the aircraft was terminated as the end of the war came into sight, and the type saw little operational use.

name: AD Navyplane
key_features: The AD Navyplane was designed by the British Admiralty's Air Department as a reconnaissance aircraft for use during World War I.  Performance of the prototype was so disappointing that plans to produc
short_description:
The AD Navyplane was designed by the British Admiralty's Air Department as a reconnaissance aircraft for use during World War I.  Performance of the prototype was so disappointing that plans to produce it were cancelled almost immediately.
The Navyplane was designed by the Admiralty's Harold Bolas with the assistance of R.J. Mitchell of Supermarine.

name: AD Scout
key_features: The AD Scout (also known as the Sparrow) was designed by Harris Booth of the British Admiralty's Air Department as a fighter aircraft to defend Britain from Zeppelin bombers during World War I.
short_description: The AD Scout (also known as the Sparrow) was designed by Harris Booth of the British Admiralty's Air Department as a fighter aircraft to defend Britain from Zeppelin bombers during World War I.

name: AD Seaplane Type 1000
key_features: The AD Seaplane Type 1000 also known as the Admiralty Type 1000 and the AD.1 (from Air Department) was a British seaplane of the First World War designed to attack German warships. When it first flew,
short_description: The AD Seaplane Type 1000 also known as the Admiralty Type 1000 and the AD.1 (from Air Department) was a British seaplane of the First World War designed to attack German warships. When it first flew, it was the largest British aircraft yet to take to the air.

name: ADI Bumble Bee
key_features: The ADI Bumble Bee (sometimes Hollmann Bumble Bee) is an ultralight gyrocopter marketed by Aircraft Designs Inc (ADI). It was the first of its kind when it flew in 1983 and is still available in plans
short_description: The ADI Bumble Bee (sometimes Hollmann Bumble Bee) is an ultralight gyrocopter marketed by Aircraft Designs Inc (ADI). It was the first of its kind when it flew in 1983 and is still available in plans form for homebuilding.

name: AEA Cygnet
key_features: The Cygnet (or Aerodrome #5) was an extremely unorthodox early Canadian aircraft, with a wall-like "wing" made up of 3,393 tetrahedral cells. It was a powered version of the Cygnet tetrahedral kite de
short_description: The Cygnet (or Aerodrome #5) was an extremely unorthodox early Canadian aircraft, with a wall-like "wing" made up of 3,393 tetrahedral cells. It was a powered version of the Cygnet tetrahedral kite designed by Dr Alexander Graham Bell in 1907 and built by the newly founded Aerial Experiment Association.

name: AEA Silver Dart
key_features: The Silver Dart (or Aerodrome #4) was a derivative of an early aircraft built by a Canadian/U.S. team, which after many successful flights in Hammondsport, New York, earlier in 1908, was dismantled an
short_description: The Silver Dart (or Aerodrome #4) was a derivative of an early aircraft built by a Canadian/U.S. team, which after many successful flights in Hammondsport, New York, earlier in 1908, was dismantled and shipped to Baddeck, Nova Scotia. It was flown from the ice of Baddeck Bay, a sub-basin of Bras d'Or Lake, on 23 February 1909, making it the first controlled powered flight in Canada.

name: AEA White Wing
key_features: The White Wing (or Aerodrome #2) was an early US aircraft designed by Frederick W. Baldwin and built by the Aerial Experiment Association in 1908. Unusual for aircraft of its day, it featured a wheele
short_description: The White Wing (or Aerodrome #2) was an early US aircraft designed by Frederick W. Baldwin and built by the Aerial Experiment Association in 1908. Unusual for aircraft of its day, it featured a wheeled undercarriage.

name: AEG (German company)
key_features: AEG Daimler-Benz Industrie AG (originally Deutsche Edison-Gesellschaft für angewandte Elektricität AG, later Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft AG, Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft AEG-Telefun
short_description:
AEG Daimler-Benz Industrie AG (originally Deutsche Edison-Gesellschaft für angewandte Elektricität AG, later Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft AG, Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft AEG-Telefunken AG, AEG-Telefunken AG and AEG AG), simply known as AEG, was a German producer of electrical equipment.
It was established in 1883 by Emil Rathenau in Berlin.

name: AEG B.II
key_features: The AEG B.II was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft produced in small numbers from 1914. It was a slightly smaller version of the B.I and proved more successful.
short_description: The AEG B.II was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft produced in small numbers from 1914. It was a slightly smaller version of the B.I and proved more successful.

name: AEG C.I
key_features: The AEG C.I was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft built by the Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft (AEG) during the First World War for the Imperial German Army's (Deutsches Heer) Imperial
short_description: The AEG C.I was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft built by the Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft (AEG) during the First World War for the Imperial German Army's (Deutsches Heer) Imperial German Air Service (Luftstreitkräfte). It was a modified version of the B.II training aircraft with a machine gun added for the observer, a more powerful engine and different wings.

name: AEG C.II
key_features: The AEG C.II was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft built by the Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft (AEG) during the First World War for the Imperial German Army's (Deutsches Heer) Imperial
short_description: The AEG C.II was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft built by the Allgemeine Elektricitäts-Gesellschaft (AEG) during the First World War for the Imperial German Army's (Deutsches Heer) Imperial German Air Service (Luftstreitkräfte). It was ordered in late 1915 as a better-performing version of the C.I resulting from a smaller and lighter structure.

name: AEG C.IV
key_features:
The AEG C.IV was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft designed and produced by the German aircraft manufacturer Allgemeine Elektrizitäts-Gesellschaft.
The C.IV was developed from the earlier AEG
short_description:
The AEG C.IV was a two-seat biplane reconnaissance aircraft designed and produced by the German aircraft manufacturer Allgemeine Elektrizitäts-Gesellschaft.

short_description: The Avro Type 688 Tudor was a British piston-engined airliner based on Avro's four-engine Lincoln bomber, itself a descendant of the famous Lancaster heavy bomber, and was Britain's first pressurised airliner. short_description: The Beechcraft Super King Air family is part of a line of twin-turboprop aircraft produced by Beechcraft. The Model 200 and Model 300 series were originally marketed as the "Super King Air" family; the "Super" designation was dropped in 1996.

short_description: The Bleriot-SPAD S.33 was a small French airliner developed soon after World War I.  The aircraft was a biplane of conventional configuration whose design owed much to the Blériot company's contemporary fighter designs such as the S.20.  Four passengers could be accommodated in an enclosed cabin within the monocoque fuselage, and a fifth passenger could ride in the open cockpit beside the pilot.

name: Blériot-SPAD S.56

short_description: The Blériot-SPAD S.56 was a family of French airliners developed in the 1920s as various refinements of the S.33 design.  All S.56 versions shared two new features: the first was a newly designed, all-metal wing, replacing the wooden wing of earlier related designs and the second was a redesigned passenger cabin, replacing the S.33's four single seats in a row with two rows of double seats.

At least 1,180 Beauforts were built by Bristol and other British manufacturers.

short_description: The Canadair North Star is a 1940s Canadian development, for Trans-Canada Air Lines (TCA), of the Douglas DC-4. short_description: The Douglas XCG-17 was an American assault glider, developed by the conversion of a C-47 Skytrain twin-engine transport during World War II. name: Aero Ae-45
key_features: The Aero 45 was a twin piston-engined civil utility aircraft produced in Czechoslovakia after World War II. Aero Vodochody produced the aircraft in 1947–1951, after which the Let Kunovice rolled out t
short_description: The Aero 45 was a twin piston-engined civil utility aircraft produced in Czechoslovakia after World War II. Aero Vodochody produced the aircraft in 1947–1951, after which the Let Kunovice rolled out these planes until 1961. In 1958 the Ae-45S became the first Czechoslovak plane to cross the Atlantic Ocean.
